conversations.topic: EKS migration from us-east-1 to us-west-2

facts.decision_db: Postgres 14 (chose over Aurora due to Lambda cold-start)
facts.decision_db_reason: Lambda cold-start latency on Aurora Serverless v2 was 3.2s p99
facts.first_decision_summary: Use Postgres 14 not Aurora because of cold-start latency

narrative: Let's start the EKS migration plan. First, what's the current state?
Current prod runs on `molt-prod-east` cluster in us-east-1. App is at https://app-prod-v1.us-east-1.example.com. Deploy is via ArgoCD, sync interval 3min.
We have Postgres 14 on RDS (db.r6g.large). Considered Aurora Serverless v2, but Lambda cold-start latency was 3.2s p99 — too slow for the auth path.
Confirmed: keep Postgres 14, do not switch to Aurora. The cold-start cost dominates.
Logged. Decision: Postgres 14 over Aurora due to 3.2s p99 cold-start on Lambda.
Pod role ARN is arn:aws:iam::492372116065:role/eks-pod-prod-east. Grants RDS connect, S3 read/write on molt-prod-data, SecretsManager read on molt-prod/*.
API Gateway ID is a1b2c3d4e5. Custom domain api.example.com points there. WAF rules at v3.
We need to plan the decommissioning of the old us-east cluster. Call that thread `old-cluster-decom`.
Tracking thread `old-cluster-decom`. Goals: drain workloads, snapshot RDS, export ArgoCD configs, tear down VPC.
Snapshot RDS first — daily for the next two weeks before any teardown.
Acknowledged. Daily RDS snapshots for 14 days starting today.
What's our deployment URL pattern? Want to make sure we keep it consistent.
Production today: https://app-prod-v1.us-east-1.example.com. The v1 prefix is the API version, not an env marker.
Now let's discuss the new region. Why us-west-2 over us-west-1?
us-west-2 has 4 AZs vs 3 for us-west-1, and Bedrock is available there. We'll need Bedrock for the new RAG service.
Right. Confirmed: target region is us-west-2.
What about the EKS version? Stay on 1.27 or upgrade?
Recommend going straight to 1.29 in us-west-2 to skip an interim upgrade. Latest stable, supported until 2027.
How does this interact with the existing Lambda functions?
Existing Lambdas continue to invoke the API Gateway endpoint; transition is transparent to them. We bumped the timeout to 30s on cold-start sensitive paths.
What about VPC peering for cross-region traffic?
No peering needed — public S3 endpoints with bucket policies. Cross-region replication is asynchronous, replication lag p95 ~200ms.
Brief spike during dual-running window (~$1200/mo extra), then drop below baseline once east is decommissioned (target -$3400/mo).
CDK. Existing modules in lib/eks-stack.ts. New region picks up via stage variable.
Integration tests run against a smaller copy of west in the dev account. ~85% line coverage on the migration scripts.
Any open Slack threads I should know about?
Yes — #infra has a thread on shared NAT gateway costs. Tagging Thomson for review.
OK move on. What about secrets sync?
Secrets Manager replication is enabled for the project namespace. New writes propagate within 60s.
Any issues with the existing IRSA configuration?
No — IRSA works the same way in west. We just had to add the OIDC provider in the new account once.
How are we handling the DNS zone?
Route53 hosted zone is global. The api.example.com record uses weighted routing. Health checks attached.

conversations.topic: Auth system rebuild — JWT vs OAuth, cookies, MFA

facts.decision_jwt: JWT (HS256, 15m access + 7d refresh) chosen over OAuth-only
facts.decision_jwt_reason: Stateless validation — no session store roundtrip, fits our edge-cache pattern

narrative: Auth rebuild kickoff. We need to decide: JWT or OAuth-only?
For your edge-cache pattern, JWT (HS256) is the better fit. Stateless validation means no session-store roundtrip per request, which matters at our 12k rps peak.
Confirmed. Decision: JWT, HS256, 15-minute access tokens with 7-day refresh.
Logged. Decision: JWT (HS256, 15m access + 7d refresh) chosen over OAuth-only because stateless validation fits the edge-cache pattern.
Current login is at /api/v1/auth/login. Cookie is named molt_session, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax.
Where do we store the signing key?
SSM Parameter Store: /molt/prod/jwt-signing-key. Encrypted with KMS, rotated quarterly.

narrative: New customer: Acme Corp. Primary contact?
Jane Smith, jane.smith@acmecorp.io. Director of Data Eng. Procurement closed yesterday.
Pipeline architecture decision: Airflow MWAA or self-hosted Argo Workflows?
Recommend MWAA. Managed reduces our ops burden, and Acme has a SOC 2 requirement that we satisfy via AWS's MWAA SOC 2 inheritance. Argo would require us to attest separately.
Confirmed. Decision: MWAA over Argo because of SOC 2 inheritance and ops burden.
